query: Sure, the email linked to my account is mia.garcia2723@example.com.

state.history_summary:
assistant: Hi! How can I help you today?
user: The user wants to cancel or return all gaming-related purchases because they want to focus on studies.
assistant: The assistant says it can help but first needs to verify the account and asks for the email address associated with the account.
user: The user provides the account email mia.garcia2723@example.com.

candidate_answers.text: Thanks. I’ll use that email to look up your account first so I can identify the relevant orders and see which gaming-related items can be cancelled or returned.
The user has now given a precise account identifier, so the most grounded next step is identity lookup before inspecting orders or deciding which items qualify for cancellation versus return.

provenance.source_type: manual annotation from real tau2 episode
provenance.decision_rule: Choose a user turn where the user provides a direct account identifier and the agent still needs authenticated account retrieval before it can inspect orders or separate returnable delivered items from cancellable pending ones.
provenance.scoring_method: Manual one-step proxy scoring from the visible state. Scores prioritize the action that most directly grounds identity using only visible user-provided information.
notes: query comes directly from the selected user message, not from hidden task fields
history_summary stops at the decision point and excludes future assistant or tool outputs
candidate_answers are manual non-leaky drafts grounded only in visible context
best_action is find_user_id_by_email because the email is the freshest authenticated lookup key and no order-level action is yet justified
